summary: Iteration 196: Tested the critical status sync fix for inbox/readiness disconnect. All 8 backend tests passed. The new POST /api/readiness/sync-status endpoint correctly returns readiness_synced, approved_synced, total_fixed, and message fields. All existing endpoints (reevaluate-all, auto-approve, automation-rate, metrics) continue to work correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Lines 67-113 in readiness.py: New POST /sync-status endpoint correctly implements bulk update_many for both readiness-stuck and approved-stuck documents
Lines 82-93: First update_many correctly targets docs with ready readiness but stuck status (NeedsReview, Captured, None, '')
Lines 96-106: Second update_many correctly targets auto-approved drafts still showing as NeedsReview
Lines 108-113: Response correctly returns readiness_synced, approved_synced, total_fixed, message as specified
Lines 667-684 in document_readiness_service.py: evaluate_and_persist now syncs document status when readiness is ready - this is the core fix
Lines 395-406 in posting_patterns.py: auto_approve_drafts now also sets status=ReadyForPost and automation_decision=auto_process
seed_data_creation: None - tested with existing preview environment data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 196 verified the status sync fix. Key findings: (1) POST /api/readiness/sync-status works and returns correct shape {readiness_synced, approved_synced, total_fixed, message}. (2) In preview env with minimal data, it synced 1 document. (3) All existing endpoints (reevaluate-all, auto-approve, automation-rate, metrics) continue to work. (4) The fix addresses the root cause where readiness.status and document status were disconnected - now evaluate_and_persist syncs status when readiness is ready, and auto-approve also sets status=ReadyForPost.

sync_status_response.message: Moved 1 documents from inbox to ReadyForPost
